title: Today's AI Brief: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
summary: A quick rundown of the latest AI news, including Trump's executive order and Google's Gemini 3.5 model.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
imageCaption: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you from the world of artificial intelligence. It's been a busy week, and I'm excited to dive in and give you the lowdown.

First up, President Trump has signed an executive order that requires AI companies to review their models before they're released to the public. The order doesn't require companies to share information with the government, but it's an interesting move to ensure that AI models are safe and trustworthy. My read is that this could be a significant step in establishing some boundaries around AI development. I'm curious to see how this plays out and what it means for the future of AI.

Also making waves is Google's new Gemini 3.5 model, which promises to bring 'frontier intelligence' to the table. According to Google, this model is designed to help execute complex workflows, which could have a major impact on how we interact with AI in the future. I am looking at this as a potential game-changer for AI adoption in industries like healthcare and finance. The possibilities seem endless, and I'm excited to see where this technology takes us.

Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Policy AI Tech Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released But it’s up to AI companies to decide whether to share information with the government. But it’s up to AI companies to decide whether to share information with the government. by Lauren Feiner Jun 2, 2026, 6:33 PM UTC Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge Lauren Feiner is a se... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action is also worth a quick mention. Koray Kavukcuoglu CTO, Google DeepMind and Chief AI Architect, Google Jeff Dean Chief Scientist, Google DeepMind and Google Research Oriol Vinyals Vice President, Google DeepMind Noam S... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
